tasks.spec:
You are a fix worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib + PyYAML only.

CONTEXT: the podcast watchdog's incident fingerprint is currently sha256('sensor|subject|failure_class')[:12]. Observed defect (2026-07-22 shadow runs): the SAME underlying problem produces different failure_class values depending on probe environment (a confined run saw 'timer_unknown' where an unconfined run saw 'timer_failed'), which mints a SECOND thread for one incident. That violates the charter's dedup priority: one incident = one thread, forever (immutable invariant escalation_dedup_policy). Also, the intent-sweeper unit's ledger only receives appends when an intent is actually due, so ledger freshness on idle days is a false 'receipt_stale'.

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: departments/podcast/runtime/fingerprint_dedup.py, departments/podcast/runtime/estate.json, departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_spine.py. Never modify anything else (sense_estate.py, compare_charter.py, escalate_outbox.py, record.py are frozen — the fingerprint change lives entirely in fingerprint_dedup.py, which is the only module that computes fingerprints; verify that claim by reading the other modules first and report in fix-summary if it is false rather than editing them).

FIX:
1. fingerprint_dedup.py — fingerprint becomes sha256('sensor|subject')[:12]. failure_class becomes a MUTABLE incident attribute: on merge, update failure_class/severity/last_seen/observed from the newest candidate, keep first_seen, increment count, union evidence (bounded to the 10 most recent entries). Keep every existing behavior: open dedup (no second thread), resolved-recurrence → department_defect, escalated-flag exactly-once semantics.
2. Migration: on load, if incidents.json contains entries whose stored fingerprint does not equal the new sensor|subject hash, collapse them: group by new fingerprint; merged incident keeps earliest first_seen, latest last_seen, max severity rank (critical>high>med>low), newest failure_class, union of evidence (bounded 10), sum of counts, escalated=True if ANY variant was escalated (prevents re-escalating a collapsed thread), state precedence: department_defect > open > resolved. Write the migrated map atomically. Migration must be idempotent (running twice changes nothing).
3. estate.json — change the obe-scheduled-intent-sweeper entry's evidence spec to "evidence": "timer_only" (drop its ledger_path): the timer+service Result probe still catches real failures; idle-day ledger staleness was a false positive.
4. tests/test_watchdog_spine.py — add: (a) stability: candidates for the same sensor+subject with different failure_class merge into ONE thread whose failure_class tracks the newest; (b) migration collapse: an incidents.json seeded with two old-keyed variants of one subject (one escalated) migrates to one incident with escalated=True and summed count, idempotent on second load; (c) sweeper idle day produces no receipt_stale (adjust any existing test that assumed ledger freshness for the sweeper). Keep ALL existing tests passing (update those that hardcode the old 3-part fingerprint input).

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/ -q

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave changes uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words).
tasks.verified: full department pytest passed including fingerprint-stability, idempotent migration-collapse, and sweeper idle-day tests; patch touches only the three owned files
